description_long: This object is a delicate, light pink flower with five broad, slightly overlapping petals that form a shallow cup shape. The petals have a soft gradient, transitioning from a pale pink at the edges to a deeper pink near the base, with subtle dark shading on the lower portions. At the center, slender yellow-tipped stamens radiate outward, adding a touch of color and detail. The flower is supported by a thin, straight, dark green stem that extends downward, with no visible leaves attached. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a cherry blossom or similar ornamental flower, often found in urban parks or landscaped areas. The structure is airy and lightweight, with a naturalistic, organic form.
description: A light pink, cup-shaped flower with five broad petals and yellow-tipped stamens, supported by a thin green stem.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open, symmetrical arrangement of five petals and the central cluster of yellow-tipped stamens.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the gentle curvature and overlapping of the petals, with more of the right side visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the outer surfaces of the petals and the subtle gradient from pink to darker tones at the base.
description_view_3: Angled view from the left, showing the petal layering and the central stamens partially obscured by the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, with five broad petals overlapping slightly at their bases and forming a shallow, symmetrical cup. The arrangement is regular and evenly spaced, typical of many ornamental flowers.
